ogTitle: Thorpdale South vs Three Bridges: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Thorpdale South and Three Bridges.
heading: Thorpdale South vs Three Bridges — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Thorpdale South, Victoria and Three Bridges, Victoria. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Three Bridges has a lower overall climate risk score (33/100) compared to Thorpdale South (45/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Thorpdale South or Three Bridges safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Thorpdale South has a flood risk score of 59/100, while Three Bridges scores 23/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Thorpdale South scores 57/100 for bushfire risk. Three Bridges scores 59/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Thorpdale South or Three Bridges?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Three Bridges has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 2.25% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
